# CMSEncoderAddRecipients(_:_:)

**Function**

Specifies a message is to be encrypted and specifies the recipients of the message.

## Declaration

```swift
func CMSEncoderAddRecipients(_ cmsEncoder: CMSEncoder, _ recipientOrArray: CFTypeRef) -> OSStatus
```

## Parameters

- **cmsEncoder**: The CMSEncoder reference returned by the `CMSEncoderCreate` function.
- **recipientOrArray**: Either a single certificate containing a public encryption key for one message recipient, specified as a certificate object (type [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecCertificate]), or a set of certificates specified as a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFArray] of certificate objects.

## Return Value

A result code. See [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/security-framework-result-codes].

## Discussion

Your keychain must contain a certificate that supports encryption for each recipient. You can call this function more than once for the same message.

You can both sign and encrypt the same message; however, you cannot call both this function and the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/CMSEncoderSetHasDetachedContent(_:_:)] function for the same message.

If you do call this function, you must call it before the first call to the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/CMSEncoderUpdateContent(_:_:_:)] function.

## Related Documentation

- **CMSEncoderUpdateContent(_:_:_:)**: Feeds content bytes into the encoder.
- **CMSEncoderCreate(_:)**: Creates a CMSEncoder reference.
- **CMSEncoderCopyRecipients(_:_:)**: Obtains the array of recipients specified with the `CMSEncoderAddRecipients` function.
- **CMSDecoderIsContentEncrypted(_:_:)**: Determines whether a CMS message was encrypted.
